sourceBio: Design @Cursor_ai. Early @NotionHQ, @Stripe, built startups. I make a world where anyone can make software. Aspiring k-pop idol.
title: Ryo Lu · @ryolu_

sentences.text: when the dream becomes the job i used to think the dream was to make my hobby my work. to spend my life close to the things i loved: computers, tools, interfaces, systems, tiny details, the feeling of making something from nothing. the things i used to do at night, after school, after work, in the margins of life, somehow became the center of my life. and it is a dream. i don’t want to pretend otherwise. there is a very specific happiness in getting paid to care. in meeting other people who care. in building a language around your obsession. in seeing something that lived only in your head become real enough for other people to touch. but there is also a quiet pain in it. because the thing that once belonged only to you no longer belongs only to you. it becomes attached to deadlines, teams, customers, strategy, money, reputation, momentum... curiosity becomes roadmap. taste becomes decision-making. play becomes output. the thing you once used to escape the world becomes the thing through which the world reaches you. and now there is another strange layer: the machines are getting good at the work too. not just the boring parts. the parts that used to feel close to the heart. writing, coding, designing, reasoning, making taste-like decisions, turning vague ideas into something real. the things we once used as proof that we had something special inside us are becoming things a model can approximate, remix, and sometimes do faster. that can feel scary in a very specific way. not only because of jobs or competition, but because it touches the myth you built around yourself. if the thing i became good at can be done by a machine, then what part of it was really me? i think that question is painful because it arrives at the same place. the original love. before the title, before the company, before the taste became a role, before the work became useful to other people, there was a small fire inside. curiosity. obsession. the feeling that the world became more alive when you understood a little more of it, or made a tiny piece of it bend to your will.

AI can make the output faster. it can even raise the floor of the craft. but it cannot want on your behalf. it cannot decide what is worth loving. it cannot protect the strange private thread that made you care in the first place. maybe that is what becomes more important now. not whether you can still produce the artifact by hand. not whether you can outperform the machine at every task. that game is already changing. the real question is whether you still have a relationship with the source. the part with no audience. no roadmap. no deadline. the part that can wander, collect strange references, make useless things. follow beauty, be wrong, be slow, be you, be true. because that part is not separate from the work. it is the source of the work. if you lose it, you can still operate. you can still manage the machine. you can still prompt, review, decide, ship, and keep things moving. but the work becomes thinner. safer. more explainable. less alive. so maybe the real discipline is not to work harder, rest more, or care less. maybe it is to keep returning to the place where the love began – before it became useful, before it became legible, before anyone needed anything from it, before even the machine could mirror it back to you. not to escape the work. to make sure the fire inside is still alive.

paragraphs.zh: 当梦想变成工作时，我过去总以为，梦想就是把自己的爱好变成职业，把一生都放在自己热爱的事物附近：computers、tools、interfaces、systems、那些微小的细节、从无到有做出东西的感觉。那些我曾经在夜里、放学后、下班后、在生活边缘做的事，不知怎么地，成了我生活的中心。而这的确是一种梦想，我不想假装不是。能因为在乎而获得报酬，有一种非常具体的幸福；能遇见其他同样在乎的人；能围绕你的痴迷建立起一种语言；能看到原本只存在于你脑海里的东西，真实到足以让别人触碰。但这其中也有一种安静的痛，因为那个曾经只属于你的东西，不再只属于你了。它开始连上 deadlines、teams、customers、strategy、money、reputation、momentum……curiosity 变成了 roadmap，taste 变成了 decision-making，play 变成了 output。那个你曾用来逃离世界的东西，变成了世界抵达你的通道。而现在又多了一层奇怪的变化：机器也越来越擅长这份工作了，而且不只是那些无聊的部分，还包括那些曾经最贴近内心的部分——writing、coding、designing、reasoning、做出像 taste 一样的判断、把模糊的想法变成真实之物。那些我们曾拿来证明自己内在有某种特别之处的能力，如今模型也可以 approximate、remix，有时甚至做得更快。这会以一种非常具体的方式让人害怕，不只是因为工作或竞争，更因为它触碰了你为自己建立的那个神话：如果我擅长的事机器也能做，那其中到底有多少才真正是“我”？我想，这个问题之所以痛，是因为它最终把你带回同一个地方：最初的热爱。在 title 之前，在 company 之前，在 taste 变成一种角色之前，在工作对别人变得有用之前，你心里先有一小团火：curiosity。obsession。那种当你多理解世界一点点，或让世界的某个微小部分稍微顺从你的意志时，世界就变得更鲜活的感觉。AI 可以让 output 更快，甚至可以抬高这门 craft 的下限，但它不能替你去渴望，不能替你决定什么值得去爱，也不能守住那条最初让你在乎的、奇怪而私人的线索。也许这正是现在变得更重要的事：不是你是否还能亲手产出 artifact，不是你是否能在每一项任务上都胜过机器——那场游戏已经在变化。真正的问题是，你是否还和源头保持关系。那个没有 audience、没有 roadmap、没有 deadline 的部分。那个可以游荡、收集奇怪参考、做无用之物的部分。去追随美，去犯错，去缓慢，去做你自己，去真实。因为那部分并不独立于工作之外，它就是工作的源头。如果你失去了它，你仍然可以运转，仍然可以管理机器，仍然可以 prompt、review、decide、ship，让一切继续向前；但工作会变得更薄、更安全、更容易解释，也更没生命力。所以，也许真正的 discipline 不是更努力地工作、更多地休息，或少在乎一点。也许它是不断回到热爱开始的地方——在它变得有用之前，在它变得可被理解之前，在任何人需要从中获得什么之前，甚至在机器都还不能把它映照回给你之前。不是为了逃离工作，而是为了确认你心里的火还活着。

sentences.text: assembling the best design team (looking for design engs, too)

paragraphs.zh: 组建最好的设计团队（也在寻找 design engs）
